ME/CFS.label: ME/CFS
ME/CFS.definition: Myalgic Encephalomyelitis / Chronic Fatigue Syndrome: a complex, chronic, multi-system neuroimmune disease characterized by severe fatigue, post-exertional malaise, unrefreshing sleep, cognitive dysfunction, and autonomic dysregulation.
ME/CFS.also: ICD-11 code 8E49; also called ME, CFS, ME/CFS, SEID

PEM.label: PEM
PEM.definition: Post-Exertional Malaise: the cardinal symptom of ME/CFS — a disproportionate and delayed worsening of all symptoms following physical, cognitive, or emotional exertion, often appearing 24–72 hours after the trigger and lasting days to weeks.
PEM.also: PENE (Post-Exertional Neuroimmune Exhaustion); PESE (Post-Exertional Symptom Exacerbation)

HRV.label: HRV
HRV.definition: Heart Rate Variability: the variation in time between successive heartbeats, reflecting autonomic nervous system balance. Reduced HRV (low parasympathetic tone) is consistently reported in ME/CFS.

AQP4.label: AQP4
AQP4.definition: Aquaporin-4: a water-channel protein highly expressed on astrocyte end-feet, essential for glymphatic clearance of brain waste products. Inhibited by sustained norepinephrine, linking sympathetic overactivation to impaired brain waste removal in ME/CFS.

TNF-α.label: TNF-α
TNF-α.definition: Tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha: a pro-inflammatory cytokine elevated in many ME/CFS patients. Promotes inflammation, sickness behavior, and sleep fragmentation via hypothalamic action.

IL-1β.label: IL-1β
IL-1β.definition: Interleukin-1 Beta: a master pro-inflammatory cytokine that mediates fever, fatigue, and sickness behavior. Elevated in subsets of ME/CFS patients and known to activate the HPA axis.

IL-6.label: IL-6
IL-6.definition: Interleukin-6: a pleiotropic cytokine with both pro- and anti-inflammatory roles. Elevated in many ME/CFS patients, associated with fatigue severity and neuroinflammation.

IFN-γ.label: IFN-γ
IFN-γ.definition: Interferon Gamma: a key Th1 cytokine involved in antiviral and immune responses. Abnormal IFN-γ signaling is found in ME/CFS immune dysfunction.

CRP.label: CRP
CRP.definition: C-Reactive Protein: an acute-phase protein produced by the liver in response to inflammation. Often within normal range in ME/CFS despite chronic inflammation, limiting its diagnostic utility.

ESR.label: ESR
ESR.definition: Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate: a non-specific marker of inflammation measuring how quickly red blood cells settle. Typically normal in ME/CFS.

NK.label: NK
NK.definition: Natural Killer cells: cytotoxic lymphocytes of the innate immune system. Reduced NK cell function (but not number) is one of the most consistently replicated immune abnormalities in ME/CFS.

HPA.label: HPA
HPA.definition: Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal axis: the central stress-response system. Blunted HPA axis responsiveness (low cortisol output) is a replicated finding in ME/CFS.

ANS.label: ANS
ANS.definition: Autonomic Nervous System: the branch of the nervous system controlling involuntary functions (heart rate, blood pressure, digestion, temperature). Dysfunction of the ANS is central to ME/CFS pathophysiology.

CNS.label: CNS
CNS.definition: Central Nervous System: the brain and spinal cord. ME/CFS involves CNS dysfunction including neuroinflammation, reduced cerebral blood flow, and cognitive impairment.

PNS.label: PNS
PNS.definition: Parasympathetic Nervous System: the 'rest and digest' branch of the autonomic nervous system. Parasympathetic tone is reduced in ME/CFS, contributing to the 'tired but wired' state.

SNS.label: SNS
SNS.definition: Sympathetic Nervous System: the 'fight or flight' branch of the autonomic nervous system. Sympathetic overactivation at rest and during sleep is a hallmark of ME/CFS.

BBB.label: BBB
BBB.definition: Blood-Brain Barrier: the highly selective semipermeable border separating circulating blood from the brain. BBB dysfunction/permeability is implicated in ME/CFS neuroinflammation.

OI.label: OI
OI.definition: Orthostatic Intolerance: the inability to maintain blood pressure and cerebral perfusion when upright. Common in ME/CFS, manifesting as dizziness, lightheadedness, palpitations, and presyncope upon standing.

POTS.label: POTS
POTS.definition: Postural Orthostatic Tachycardia Syndrome: a form of orthostatic intolerance defined by a heart rate increase of ≥30 BPM (≥40 in adolescents) within 10 minutes of standing, without orthostatic hypotension. Frequent comorbidity of ME/CFS.

NMH.label: NMH
NMH.definition: Neurally Mediated Hypotension: a form of orthostatic intolerance where blood pressure drops after prolonged standing due to a neurally mediated reflex. Also called vasovagal syncope or neurocardiogenic syncope.

MCAS.label: MCAS
MCAS.definition: Mast Cell Activation Syndrome: a condition of inappropriate mast cell degranulation causing multi-system symptoms (flushing, itching, GI distress, tachycardia, wheezing). Frequently comorbid with ME/CFS and POTS.

MCS.label: MCS
MCS.definition: Multiple Chemical Sensitivity: a condition of heightened reactivity to low levels of chemicals (fragrances, solvents, cleaning products). Reported comorbidity in a subset of ME/CFS patients.

FM.label: FM
FM.definition: Fibromyalgia: a chronic condition of widespread pain, fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, and sleep disturbance. Shares substantial clinical overlap with ME/CFS; ~35–70% of ME/CFS patients also meet FM criteria.

IBS.label: IBS
IBS.definition: Irritable Bowel Syndrome: a functional gastrointestinal disorder characterized by abdominal pain, bloating, and altered bowel habits. Highly prevalent in ME/CFS (30–60%).

IBD.label: IBD
IBD.definition: Inflammatory Bowel Disease: a group of chronic inflammatory conditions of the GI tract (Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis). Overlaps with ME/CFS in symptom burden but is a distinct inflammatory pathology.

GERD.label: GERD
GERD.definition: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease: chronic acid reflux from the stomach into the esophagus. Common comorbidity in ME/CFS.

EDS.label: EDS
EDS.definition: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome: a group of inherited connective tissue disorders characterized by joint hypermobility, skin hyperextensibility, and tissue fragility. Hypermobile EDS (hEDS) is the most common subtype co-occurring with ME/CFS.

HSD.label: HSD
HSD.definition: Hypermobility Spectrum Disorder: a connective tissue disorder characterized by joint hypermobility with associated symptoms (pain, fatigue, GI dysfunction, dysautonomia). Closely related to and often co-occurring with ME/CFS.

SFN.label: SFN
SFN.definition: Small Fiber Neuropathy: damage to small unmyelinated nerve fibers (Aδ and C fibers), causing neuropathic pain, burning, tingling, and autonomic symptoms. Identified in a significant subset of ME/CFS patients via skin biopsy.

CCI.label: CCI
CCI.definition: Craniocervical Instability: abnormal movement at the junction of the skull and upper cervical spine, potentially compressing the brainstem and causing neurological symptoms. A controversial but recognized finding in a subset of severe ME/CFS patients.

AAI.label: AAI
AAI.definition: Atlantoaxial Instability: excessive movement between the first and second cervical vertebrae (C1–C2). Often coexists with CCI in ME/CFS patients with connective tissue disorders.

ICP.label: ICP
ICP.definition: Intracranial Pressure: the pressure inside the skull and on brain tissue. Abnormal ICP dynamics have been hypothesized in ME/CFS, particularly in the context of CCI.

IIH.label: IIH
IIH.definition: Idiopathic Intracranial Hypertension: elevated intracranial pressure of unknown cause, producing headache, visual disturbances, and pulsatile tinnitus. May overlap with ME/CFS symptomatology.

CSF.label: CSF
CSF.definition: Cerebrospinal Fluid: the clear fluid surrounding the brain and spinal cord. ME/CFS CSF studies show elevated cytokines, lactate, and other metabolic abnormalities.

VEGF.label: VEGF
VEGF.definition: Vascular Endothelial Growth Factor: a signaling protein that stimulates blood vessel formation. Elevated VEGF has been reported in ME/CFS, potentially reflecting endothelial dysfunction.

TGF-β.label: TGF-β
TGF-β.definition: Transforming Growth Factor Beta: a cytokine with immunosuppressive and pro-fibrotic functions. Elevated in some ME/CFS studies, associated with Th2/Th17 skewing.

BDNF.label: BDNF
BDNF.definition: Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor: a protein supporting neuronal survival, growth, and synaptic plasticity. Altered BDNF levels have been reported in ME/CFS.

NGF.label: NGF
NGF.definition: Nerve Growth Factor: a neurotrophin important for sensory and sympathetic neuron survival. Elevated NGF may contribute to pain and hyperalgesia in ME/CFS.

ATP.label: ATP
ATP.definition: Adenosine Triphosphate: the primary energy currency of cells. Impaired ATP production via mitochondrial dysfunction is a central hypothesis in ME/CFS energy failure.

ADP.label: ADP
ADP.definition: Adenosine Diphosphate: the product of ATP hydrolysis. Elevated ADP/ATP ratios in ME/CFS indicate impaired energy metabolism.

AMP.label: AMP
AMP.definition: Adenosine Monophosphate: a nucleotide and energy-sensing molecule. Elevated AMP activates AMPK, the cellular energy sensor frequently implicated in ME/CFS metabolic dysfunction.

NAD+.label: NAD+
NAD+.definition: Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide (oxidized): a critical coenzyme in energy metabolism (glycolysis, TCA cycle, OXPHOS). NAD+ depletion is hypothesized as a key metabolic bottleneck in ME/CFS.

NADPH.label: NADPH
NADPH.definition: Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide Phosphate (reduced): essential for antioxidant defense (glutathione recycling) and biosynthetic reactions. NADPH depletion may impair redox balance in ME/CFS.

FAD.label: FAD
FAD.definition: Flavin Adenine Dinucleotide: a redox cofactor in the electron transport chain (Complex II) and fatty acid oxidation. FAD-dependent enzymes are critical to mitochondrial energy production.

ROS.label: ROS
ROS.definition: Reactive Oxygen Species: chemically reactive oxygen-containing molecules (superoxide, hydrogen peroxide, hydroxyl radical). Excessive ROS production (oxidative stress) is a consistent finding in ME/CFS.

RNS.label: RNS
RNS.definition: Reactive Nitrogen Species: nitrogen-containing free radicals (peroxynitrite, nitric oxide). Combined ROS/RNS stress contributes to nitric oxide–peroxynitrite cycle dysfunction in ME/CFS.

NOS.label: NOS
NOS.definition: Nitric Oxide Synthase: the enzyme family producing nitric oxide. Dysregulation of NOS isoforms (iNOS, eNOS, nNOS) is implicated in ME/CFS vascular and inflammatory pathology.

ONOO.label: ONOO
ONOO.definition: Peroxynitrite: a potent oxidant formed by the reaction of nitric oxide with superoxide. Central to the NO/ONOO cycle hypothesis of ME/CFS pathophysiology.

COX.label: COX
COX.definition: Cyclooxygenase: enzyme producing prostaglandins from arachidonic acid. COX-2 is induced by inflammation; implicated in ME/CFS neuroinflammation and pain.

LOX.label: LOX
LOX.definition: Lipoxygenase: enzyme producing leukotrienes and other inflammatory mediators from arachidonic acid. LOX pathway activation contributes to oxidative stress in ME/CFS.

MAPK.label: MAPK
MAPK.definition: Mitogen-Activated Protein Kinase: a signaling pathway regulating cell proliferation, differentiation, and stress responses. MAPK/ERK and p38 MAPK activation have been reported in ME/CFS immune cells.

ERK.label: ERK
ERK.definition: Extracellular signal-Regulated Kinase: a MAPK subfamily involved in growth factor signaling. Abnormal ERK activation may contribute to immune dysfunction in ME/CFS.

JNK.label: JNK
JNK.definition: c-Jun N-terminal Kinase: a stress-activated MAPK responding to cytokines, oxidative stress, and ER stress. JNK activation may drive inflammation and apoptosis in ME/CFS.

NF-κB.label: NF-κB
NF-κB.definition: Nuclear Factor Kappa B: a master transcription factor driving pro-inflammatory gene expression. Chronic NF-κB activation is a candidate mechanism for sustained neuroinflammation in ME/CFS.

mTOR.label: mTOR
mTOR.definition: Mechanistic Target of Rapamycin: a central nutrient/energy sensor regulating cell growth and metabolism. mTOR dysregulation is linked to ME/CFS metabolic inflexibility.

AMPK.label: AMPK
AMPK.definition: AMP-Activated Protein Kinase: the cellular energy sensor activated by rising AMP/ATP ratios. Impaired AMPK activation may prevent appropriate energy-conservation responses in ME/CFS.

PGC-1α.label: PGC-1α
PGC-1α.definition: Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor Gamma Coactivator 1-Alpha: the master regulator of mitochondrial biogenesis. Reduced PGC-1α expression may underlie mitochondrial impairment in ME/CFS.

SIRT.label: SIRT
SIRT.definition: Sirtuins: a family of NAD+-dependent deacetylase enzymes regulating metabolism, stress resistance, and aging. SIRT1 and SIRT3 dysfunction may contribute to ME/CFS metabolic dysregulation.

NRF2.label: NRF2
NRF2.definition: Nuclear factor Erythroid 2-Related Factor 2: the master regulator of antioxidant gene expression. Impaired NRF2 activation leads to reduced antioxidant defenses in ME/CFS.

SOD.label: SOD
SOD.definition: Superoxide Dismutase: the primary antioxidant enzyme converting superoxide to hydrogen peroxide. Reduced SOD activity is reported in ME/CFS.

GPX.label: GPX
GPX.definition: Glutathione Peroxidase: a selenium-dependent enzyme reducing hydrogen peroxide and lipid hydroperoxides, using glutathione. GPX dysfunction impairs antioxidant defense in ME/CFS.

GSH.label: GSH
GSH.definition: Glutathione (reduced): the body's master intracellular antioxidant. Reduced GSH and low GSH/GSSG ratios are frequently reported in ME/CFS, indicating oxidative stress.

GSSG.label: GSSG
GSSG.definition: Glutathione Disulfide (oxidized): the oxidized form of glutathione. Elevated GSSG/GSH ratios indicate oxidative stress, a consistent finding in ME/CFS.

MDA.label: MDA
MDA.definition: Malondialdehyde: a marker of lipid peroxidation produced by oxidative damage to cell membranes. Elevated MDA is a consistent finding in ME/CFS oxidative stress research.

GABA.label: GABA
GABA.definition: Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid: the brain's primary inhibitory neurotransmitter. GABAergic dysfunction may contribute to sleep disturbance, anxiety, and sensory hypersensitivity in ME/CFS.

NMDA.label: NMDA
NMDA.definition: N-Methyl-D-Aspartate receptor: a glutamate receptor subtype involved in synaptic plasticity, learning, and excitotoxicity. NMDA receptor overactivation is implicated in ME/CFS central sensitization and neurotoxicity.

DA.label: DA
DA.definition: Dopamine: a catecholamine neurotransmitter involved in motivation, reward, motor control, and cognitive function. Dopaminergic dysfunction is implicated in ME/CFS fatigue and cognitive symptoms.

NE.label: NE
NE.definition: Norepinephrine (Noradrenaline): a catecholamine neurotransmitter and hormone driving sympathetic nervous system activation. Sustained elevated NE at night is a hallmark of autonomic dysfunction in ME/CFS.

5-HT.label: 5-HT
5-HT.definition: 5-Hydroxytryptamine (Serotonin): a monoamine neurotransmitter involved in mood, sleep, appetite, pain perception, and GI function. Serotonergic dysfunction is implicated in multiple ME/CFS symptom domains.

ACh.label: ACh
ACh.definition: Acetylcholine: the primary parasympathetic neurotransmitter. Reduced cholinergic (vagal) tone contributes to autonomic imbalance in ME/CFS.

CRH.label: CRH
CRH.definition: Corticotropin-Releasing Hormone: hypothalamic hormone triggering ACTH release from the pituitary. CRH dysregulation is part of the blunted HPA axis in ME/CFS.

ACTH.label: ACTH
ACTH.definition: Adrenocorticotropic Hormone: pituitary hormone stimulating cortisol release from the adrenal glands. Blunted ACTH responses contribute to low cortisol output in ME/CFS.

GH.label: GH
GH.definition: Growth Hormone: pituitary hormone supporting tissue repair, metabolism, and immune function. Altered GH secretion, especially nocturnal GH, has been reported in ME/CFS.

IGF-1.label: IGF-1
IGF-1.definition: Insulin-like Growth Factor 1: hormone mediating many GH effects. Reduced IGF-1 has been reported in subsets of ME/CFS patients.

TSH.label: TSH
TSH.definition: Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone: pituitary hormone controlling thyroid hormone production. TSH is commonly tested but often normal in ME/CFS despite symptoms resembling hypothyroidism.

DHEA.label: DHEA
DHEA.definition: Dehydroepiandrosterone: an adrenal steroid hormone precursor. Low DHEA-S levels have been reported in ME/CFS, reflecting adrenal dysfunction.

RAAS.label: RAAS
RAAS.definition: Renin-Angiotensin-Aldosterone System: the hormonal system regulating blood pressure, fluid balance, and vascular tone. RAAS dysfunction contributes to orthostatic intolerance and low blood volume in ME/CFS.

ACE.label: ACE
ACE.definition: Angiotensin-Converting Enzyme: converts angiotensin I to angiotensin II in the RAAS. ACE dysregulation may contribute to vascular and autonomic dysfunction in ME/CFS.

ANP.label: ANP
ANP.definition: Atrial Natriuretic Peptide: heart-derived hormone promoting sodium excretion and vasodilation. ANP levels may be altered in ME/CFS-related dysautonomia.

BNP.label: BNP
BNP.definition: B-Type Natriuretic Peptide: ventricular hormone responding to cardiac stretch. Used clinically to assess heart failure; generally normal in ME/CFS.

RBC.label: RBC
RBC.definition: Red Blood Cells (Erythrocytes): oxygen-carrying blood cells. RBC deformability and morphology abnormalities have been reported in ME/CFS.

PMC.label: PMC
PMC.definition: Post-Masking Crash: a now-withdrawn concept proposed by Chang et al. (2025) suggesting that unmasked PEM — the natural crash after overexertion — somehow differs from 'true' PEM, based on a methodologically flawed survey using non-validated recall items and no objective PEM provocation. No clinical or physiological basis exists for this distinction; PEM is the same phenomenon regardless of the triggering context. The terminology risked implying that patients who must push through activities (work, caregiving, medical appointments) are not experiencing authentic PEM — an implication at odds with the biomedical consensus that PEM is a single, objectively demonstrable pathophysiological response to exertion exceeding individual capacity.
PMC.also: Withdrawn concept (Chang et al. 2025); see PEM for the validated, consensus definition

WBC.label: WBC
WBC.definition: White Blood Cells (Leukocytes): immune cells including neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, eosinophils, basophils. WBC counts are generally normal in ME/CFS.

Hb.label: Hb
Hb.definition: Hemoglobin: the iron-containing oxygen-carrying protein in red blood cells. Normal in ME/CFS but occasionally low due to comorbid iron deficiency.

GWAS.label: GWAS
GWAS.definition: Genome-Wide Association Study: a method scanning the entire genome for genetic variants associated with a disease. GWAS in ME/CFS have identified suggestive loci but no definitive genetic marker.

SNP.label: SNP
SNP.definition: Single Nucleotide Polymorphism: a single base-pair variation in DNA. Certain SNPs (e.g., MTHFR, COMT) have been investigated for association with ME/CFS.

MTHFR.label: MTHFR
MTHFR.definition: Methylenetetrahydrofolate Reductase: enzyme critical to folate metabolism and methylation. MTHFR polymorphisms (e.g., C677T) have been investigated in ME/CFS for methylation cycle dysfunction.

COMT.label: COMT
COMT.definition: Catechol-O-Methyltransferase: enzyme degrading catecholamine neurotransmitters (dopamine, norepinephrine, epinephrine). COMT polymorphisms (e.g., Val158Met) may influence pain sensitivity and cognitive function in ME/CFS.

MAO.label: MAO
MAO.definition: Monoamine Oxidase: enzyme breaking down monoamine neurotransmitters (serotonin, dopamine, norepinephrine). MAO inhibitors (MAOIs) are a class of antidepressants rarely used in ME/CFS.
MAO.also: MAO-A (preferentially degrades serotonin, NE); MAO-B (preferentially degrades dopamine)

NET.label: NET
NET.definition: Norepinephrine Transporter: protein reuptaking norepinephrine from synapses. NET dysfunction may contribute to sympathetic overactivity in ME/CFS.

SERT.label: SERT
SERT.definition: Serotonin Transporter: protein reuptaking serotonin from synapses. Targeted by SSRIs; SERT polymorphisms (5-HTTLPR) have been studied in ME/CFS.

DAT.label: DAT
DAT.definition: Dopamine Transporter: protein reuptaking dopamine from synapses. Reduced DAT availability in the striatum has been reported in ME/CFS neuroimaging.

TH.label: TH
TH.definition: Tyrosine Hydroxylase: the rate-limiting enzyme in catecholamine synthesis (converts tyrosine to L-DOPA). TH dysregulation may contribute to norepinephrine excess in ME/CFS.

TPH.label: TPH
TPH.definition: Tryptophan Hydroxylase: the rate-limiting enzyme in serotonin synthesis (converts tryptophan to 5-HTP). TPH dysregulation may alter serotonin levels in ME/CFS.

IDO.label: IDO
IDO.definition: Indoleamine 2,3-Dioxygenase: enzyme diverting tryptophan metabolism from serotonin to the kynurenine pathway. IDO is induced by IFN-γ and inflammation; IDO activation is linked to ME/CFS tryptophan depletion and neurotoxicity.

TDO.label: TDO
TDO.definition: Tryptophan 2,3-Dioxygenase: liver enzyme initiating the kynurenine pathway. Together with IDO, TDO activation shifts tryptophan away from serotonin/melatonin toward neurotoxic metabolites in ME/CFS.

KYN.label: KYN
KYN.definition: Kynurenine: the first intermediate of the kynurenine pathway from tryptophan. The KYN/tryptophan ratio is a marker of IDO activation and immune dysfunction in ME/CFS.

KYNA.label: KYNA
KYNA.definition: Kynurenic Acid: a neuroprotective metabolite of the kynurenine pathway acting as an NMDA receptor antagonist. The KYNA/QUIN ratio determines neuroprotection vs neurotoxicity balance in ME/CFS.

QUIN.label: QUIN
QUIN.definition: Quinolinic Acid: a neurotoxic kynurenine metabolite and NMDA receptor agonist. Elevated QUIN promotes excitotoxicity and neuroinflammation; QUIN/KYNA imbalance is hypothesized in ME/CFS.

LDH.label: LDH
LDH.definition: Lactate Dehydrogenase: enzyme interconverting pyruvate and lactate. Elevated LDH may reflect metabolic shift toward anaerobic glycolysis in ME/CFS.

CK.label: CK
CK.definition: Creatine Kinase: enzyme involved in ATP regeneration in muscle. Generally normal in ME/CFS despite severe fatigue, distinguishing it from primary muscle diseases.

AST.label: AST
AST.definition: Aspartate Aminotransferase: liver enzyme commonly measured in blood panels. Generally normal in ME/CFS; elevated AST suggests other pathology.

ALT.label: ALT
ALT.definition: Alanine Aminotransferase: liver enzyme measured in standard blood panels. Normal in ME/CFS despite fatigue and malaise.

GFR.label: GFR
GFR.definition: Glomerular Filtration Rate: measure of kidney function. eGFR (estimated GFR) is typically normal in ME/CFS.

RA.label: RA
RA.definition: Rheumatoid Arthritis: an autoimmune inflammatory arthritis. Joint pain and fatigue symptoms may overlap with ME/CFS; RA is a differential diagnosis.

SLE.label: SLE
SLE.definition: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus: a systemic autoimmune disease affecting multiple organs. Fatigue is a dominant symptom; SLE must be ruled out before diagnosing ME/CFS.

MS.label: MS
MS.definition: Multiple Sclerosis: a demyelinating autoimmune disease of the CNS. Fatigue is the most common symptom of MS; MS is a key differential diagnosis for ME/CFS.

PD.label: PD
PD.definition: Parkinson's Disease: a neurodegenerative disorder of the dopaminergic system. Fatigue and autonomic dysfunction overlap with ME/CFS symptoms.

ALS.label: ALS
ALS.definition: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis: a progressive neurodegenerative disease of motor neurons. Severe fatigue can be a prodromal feature.

AD.label: AD
AD.definition: Alzheimer's Disease: the most common neurodegenerative dementia. Not a comorbidity of ME/CFS, but relevant to glymphatic clearance research.

PTSD.label: PTSD
PTSD.definition: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder: a psychiatric condition following trauma with hyperarousal and avoidance. Higher prevalence in ME/CFS; trauma history may influence disease expression.

GAD.label: GAD
GAD.definition: Generalized Anxiety Disorder: chronic excessive worry and anxiety. Higher prevalence in ME/CFS; may be secondary to autonomic hyperarousal.
GAD.also: Not to be confused with the enzyme GAD (glutamic acid decarboxylase)

MDD.label: MDD
MDD.definition: Major Depressive Disorder: mood disorder characterized by persistent low mood and anhedonia. Depression is a common comorbidity but ME/CFS is a distinct disease entity with distinct pathophysiology.

OCD.label: OCD
OCD.definition: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder: a condition of intrusive thoughts and compulsive behaviors. Reported at elevated rates in ME/CFS cohorts.

ADHD.label: ADHD
ADHD.definition: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder: a neurodevelopmental condition of inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity. Associated with ME/CFS via shared dopaminergic dysfunction and increased prevalence.

ASD.label: ASD
ASD.definition: Autism Spectrum Disorder: a neurodevelopmental condition of social communication differences and restricted/repetitive behaviors. Higher prevalence in ME/CFS cohorts; both conditions share sensory sensitivities and autonomic dysregulation.

EBV.label: EBV
EBV.definition: Epstein-Barr Virus: a herpesvirus causing infectious mononucleosis. EBV reactivation is one of the most studied infectious triggers of ME/CFS onset.

HHV-6.label: HHV-6
HHV-6.definition: Human Herpesvirus 6: a herpesvirus commonly infecting children (roseola). HHV-6 reactivation has been linked to ME/CFS onset and severity.

CMV.label: CMV
CMV.definition: Cytomegalovirus: a common herpesvirus. CMV seropositivity and reactivation have been investigated in ME/CFS cohorts.

HSV.label: HSV
HSV.definition: Herpes Simplex Virus: HSV-1 (oral) and HSV-2 (genital). Reactivation may contribute to ME/CFS symptom flares.

VZV.label: VZV
VZV.definition: Varicella Zoster Virus: causes chickenpox and shingles. VZV reactivation is a possible trigger or contributor to ME/CFS.

SARS-CoV-2.label: SARS-CoV-2
SARS-CoV-2.definition: Severe Acute Respiratory Syndrome Coronavirus 2: the virus causing COVID-19. A major trigger for post-infectious ME/CFS-like illness (Long COVID / PASC), with ~50% of Long COVID patients meeting ME/CFS diagnostic criteria.

HIV.label: HIV
HIV.definition: Human Immunodeficiency Virus: the virus causing AIDS. HIV-associated fatigue and ME/CFS share overlapping features; HIV must be excluded before ME/CFS diagnosis.

PASC.label: PASC
PASC.definition: Post-Acute Sequelae of SARS-CoV-2 infection (Long COVID): persistent symptoms following COVID-19, with substantial clinical and biological overlap with ME/CFS.

LC.label: LC
LC.definition: Long COVID: shorthand for PASC. Approximately 43–58% of Long COVID patients meet ME/CFS criteria; the two conditions share PEM, orthostatic intolerance, cognitive dysfunction, and metabolic abnormalities.

SIBO.label: SIBO
SIBO.definition: Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth: excessive bacterial colonization of the small intestine causing bloating, malabsorption, and systemic symptoms. Highly prevalent in ME/CFS (reported in 40–70% of patients).

FMT.label: FMT
FMT.definition: Fecal Microbiota Transplantation: transfer of gut microbiota from a healthy donor to a patient. Investigated experimentally for ME/CFS gut microbiome dysfunction.

CBT.label: CBT
CBT.definition: Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy: a psychological intervention. No longer recommended by NICE as a curative treatment for ME/CFS; may have supportive role for coping with chronic illness but does not address underlying pathophysiology.

GET.label: GET
GET.definition: Graded Exercise Therapy: a progressive exercise program. Contraindicated in ME/CFS — NICE (2021) explicitly recommends against GET due to risk of harm (PEM exacerbation). Distinct from pacing/energy management.

LDN.label: LDN
LDN.definition: Low-Dose Naltrexone (1.5–4.5 mg/day): an opioid antagonist with paradoxical anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects at low doses, via transient opioid receptor blockade and toll-like receptor 4 (TLR4) antagonism on microglia. Used off-label for ME/CFS, fibromyalgia, and autoimmune conditions.
LDN.brand: Revia, Vivitrol (standard dose); compounded for LDN
LDN.rx: Prescription (US, EU); must be compounded for LDN dosing

IVIG.label: IVIG
IVIG.definition: Intravenous Immunoglobulin: pooled antibodies from thousands of donors, administered intravenously. Immunomodulatory; investigated in ME/CFS for immune dysfunction and autoantibody-mediated subsets. Expensive and supply-limited.
IVIG.rx: Prescription only; restricted to hospital/infusion center administration

SCIG.label: SCIG
SCIG.definition: Subcutaneous Immunoglobulin: same as IVIG but administered under the skin, allowing home self-administration. Used in ME/CFS where IVIG shows benefit but infusion center access is a barrier.

NAC.label: NAC
NAC.definition: N-Acetyl Cysteine: a precursor to glutathione, the body's master antioxidant. Used in ME/CFS for oxidative stress; also shows mucolytic, anti-inflammatory, and glutamate-modulating effects.
NAC.rx: OTC (as supplement); prescription for inhalation form

CoQ10.label: CoQ10
CoQ10.definition: Coenzyme Q10 (Ubiquinone/Ubiquinol): a critical component of the mitochondrial electron transport chain (Complex I–II shuttle) and a lipid-soluble antioxidant. CoQ10 levels are reduced in ME/CFS; supplementation is a common mitochondrial support strategy.

HBOT.label: HBOT
HBOT.definition: Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy: breathing 100% oxygen at elevated atmospheric pressure. Investigated in ME/CFS for mitochondrial support, tissue oxygenation, and anti-inflammatory effects. Evidence is limited.
HBOT.rx: Prescription only (medical grade); also available at private clinics

TMS.label: TMS
TMS.definition: Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation: non-invasive brain stimulation using magnetic fields. Repetitive TMS (rTMS) has been explored for ME/CFS cognitive dysfunction and depression, with preliminary positive signals.

VNS.label: VNS
VNS.definition: Vagus Nerve Stimulation: electrical stimulation of the vagus nerve to increase parasympathetic tone. Non-invasive transcutaneous VNS (tVNS) devices are being investigated for ME/CFS autonomic dysfunction.
VNS.rx: Implantable: prescription only. Non-invasive tVNS: some OTC devices available

CPET.label: CPET
CPET.definition: Cardiopulmonary Exercise Testing: a maximal exercise test measuring gas exchange (VO₂, VCO₂), heart rate, and ventilatory parameters. Two-day CPET (2-day CPET) uniquely demonstrates reduced VO₂ peak and anaerobic threshold on day 2 in ME/CFS — a physiological signature of PEM.

VO2max.label: VO2max
VO2max.definition: Maximal Oxygen Uptake: the maximum rate of oxygen consumption during exercise, reflecting cardiorespiratory fitness. Reduced in ME/CFS, particularly on repeat testing (2-day CPET).

MRI.label: MRI
MRI.definition: Magnetic Resonance Imaging: neuroimaging showing structural and functional brain abnormalities in ME/CFS, including white matter changes, reduced gray matter volume, and altered connectivity.

fMRI.label: fMRI
fMRI.definition: Functional MRI: brain imaging measuring activity via blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) signal. ME/CFS fMRI studies show altered connectivity and reduced cerebral blood flow during cognitive tasks.

SPECT.label: SPECT
SPECT.definition: Single Photon Emission Computed Tomography: nuclear imaging of cerebral blood flow. SPECT studies consistently show reduced cerebral perfusion in ME/CFS, particularly in the brainstem and frontal lobes.

PET.label: PET
PET.definition: Positron Emission Tomography: nuclear imaging of metabolic activity. PET studies in ME/CFS show neuroinflammation (microglial activation) and metabolic abnormalities.

CT.label: CT
CT.definition: Computed Tomography: X-ray based cross-sectional imaging. Generally normal in ME/CFS; used to exclude structural CNS pathology.

MEG.label: MEG
MEG.definition: Magnetoencephalography: technique mapping brain activity by recording magnetic fields from neuronal currents. MEG has been used to study altered cortical excitability in ME/CFS.

NIRS.label: NIRS
NIRS.definition: Near-Infrared Spectroscopy: non-invasive optical technique measuring tissue oxygenation. Used in ME/CFS to assess cerebral and muscle oxygenation during orthostatic challenge and exercise.

ECG.label: ECG
ECG.definition: Electrocardiogram: recording of the heart's electrical activity. ECG may show tachycardia (resting sinus tachycardia), but structurally normal in ME/CFS. Used in POTS diagnosis via standing test or tilt table.

EEG.label: EEG
EEG.definition: Electroencephalogram: recording of brain electrical activity. EEG in ME/CFS may show alpha intrusion into delta sleep (the 'alpha-delta anomaly'), correlating with unrefreshing sleep.

EMG.label: EMG
EMG.definition: Electromyography: test of muscle electrical activity. Generally normal in ME/CFS despite severe fatigue, distinguishing it from primary neuromuscular disorders.

BMI.label: BMI
BMI.definition: Body Mass Index: weight-to-height ratio. Not a diagnostic tool for ME/CFS; however, weight changes (gain or loss) can occur due to reduced activity, metabolic shifts, or comorbid conditions.

BP.label: BP
BP.definition: Blood Pressure: the pressure of circulating blood on vessel walls. Orthostatic BP changes are central to ME/CFS autonomic assessment; many patients show either orthostatic hypotension or a blunted/nocturnal BP dip.

ICC.label: ICC
ICC.definition: International Consensus Criteria (2011): ME diagnostic criteria requiring post-exertional neuroimmune exhaustion (PENE) plus neurological, immune, and energy metabolism impairments. More specific than Fukuda/CDC but excludes patients with primary psychiatric disorders.

CCC.label: CCC
CCC.definition: Canadian Consensus Criteria (2003): ME/CFS diagnostic criteria requiring PEM plus multiple symptom domains (fatigue, sleep, pain, neurological/cognitive, autonomic, neuroendocrine, immune). Widely used in research.

SEID.label: SEID
SEID.definition: Systemic Exertion Intolerance Disease: the IOM (2015) proposed name for ME/CFS, emphasizing PEM and systemic dysfunction. Adopted by some researchers but not widely accepted by patients or clinicians.

ICD-10.label: ICD-10
ICD-10.definition: International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision. ME/CFS classified under G93.3 (postviral fatigue syndrome / benign myalgic encephalomyelitis).

ICD-11.label: ICD-11
ICD-11.definition: International Classification of Diseases, 11th Revision. ME/CFS classified under 8E49 (postviral fatigue syndrome) in the diseases of the nervous system chapter, reinforcing its neurological classification.

NICE.label: NICE
NICE.definition: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (UK): published landmark ME/CFS guidelines (NG206, 2021) recommending against GET, emphasizing pacing/energy management, and recognizing ME/CFS as a complex multi-system disease.

IOM.label: IOM
IOM.definition: Institute of Medicine (US, now National Academy of Medicine): published the 2015 report 'Beyond Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome', proposing SEID criteria and affirming ME/CFS as a serious biomedical disease.

CDC.label: CDC
CDC.definition: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (US): the US public health agency responsible for ME/CFS surveillance, research funding, and clinical guidance (Fukuda 1994 criteria).

WHO.label: WHO
WHO.definition: World Health Organization: the UN agency classifying ME/CFS in ICD-10 (G93.3) and ICD-11 (8E49) under neurological disorders.

NIH.label: NIH
NIH.definition: National Institutes of Health (US): the primary US biomedical research agency. The NIH intramural ME/CFS study (2016–2024) provided key immunological, metabolic, and neurological findings.

OMF.label: OMF
OMF.definition: Open Medicine Foundation: a non-profit funding and coordinating ME/CFS biomedical research, including the Severely Ill Patient Study and the Harvard ME/CFS Collaboration.

RCT.label: RCT
RCT.definition: Randomized Controlled Trial: the gold standard study design for evaluating treatment efficacy. Few large RCTs exist in ME/CFS; sample sizes are typically small.

SSRI.label: SSRI
SSRI.definition: Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitor: antidepressant class blocking serotonin reuptake (fluoxetine, sertraline, citalopram, escitalopram, paroxetine). Used in ME/CFS for comorbid depression/anxiety and pain; does not treat core ME/CFS pathology.

SNRI.label: SNRI
SNRI.definition: Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitor: antidepressant class blocking both serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake (duloxetine, venlafaxine, milnacipran). More relevant to ME/CFS pain and fatigue than SSRIs due to norepinephrine component.

TCA.label: TCA
TCA.definition: Tricyclic Antidepressant: older antidepressant class (amitriptyline, nortriptyline). Used at low doses in ME/CFS for sleep, pain, and headache prophylaxis. Low-dose amitriptyline is one of the most commonly prescribed ME/CFS medications.

NSAID.label: NSAID
NSAID.definition: Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drug: class of analgesics reducing inflammation via COX inhibition (ibuprofen, naproxen, celecoxib, diclofenac). Used symptomatically in ME/CFS for pain and inflammation; limited by GI and renal side effects.
NSAID.rx: Some OTC (ibuprofen, naproxen), some prescription (celecoxib, diclofenac)

T3.label: T3
T3.definition: Triiodothyronine: the active thyroid hormone. T3 levels may be low-normal in ME/CFS; some clinicians trial low-dose T3 for persistent hypothyroid-like symptoms despite normal TSH.

T4.label: T4
T4.definition: Thyroxine: the primary thyroid hormone (prohormone), converted to active T3 in tissues. Standard thyroid replacement for hypothyroidism; typically normal in ME/CFS.

BCAA.label: BCAA
BCAA.definition: Branched-Chain Amino Acids: leucine, isoleucine, and valine. BCAAs may be depleted in ME/CFS due to catabolic metabolism and impaired energy production; supplementation is experimental.

GTPCH.label: GTPCH
GTPCH.definition: GTP Cyclohydrolase I: the rate-limiting enzyme in tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) synthesis. GTPCH dysfunction depletes BH4, linking to reduced nitric oxide, dopamine, and serotonin synthesis — a key node in ME/CFS metabolic dysfunction.

BH4.label: BH4
BH4.definition: Tetrahydrobiopterin: an essential cofactor for nitric oxide synthase, tyrosine hydroxylase, tryptophan hydroxylase, and phenylalanine hydroxylase. BH4 depletion is a convergent mechanism linking vascular, neurotransmitter, and inflammatory dysfunction in ME/CFS.

MBSR.label: MBSR
MBSR.definition: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction: an 8-week structured program teaching mindfulness meditation. Used supportively in ME/CFS for coping and symptom management, though effects on core pathophysiology are unclear.

IV.label: IV
IV.definition: Intravenous: medication administration directly into a vein. IV therapies in ME/CFS include IVIG, saline infusions (for POTS/hypovolemia), and vitamin infusions.

OTC.label: OTC
OTC.definition: Over-The-Counter: medications and supplements available without a prescription. Many ME/CFS supplements (CoQ10, NAC, BCAAs, vitamins) are OTC.

FDA.label: FDA
FDA.definition: US Food and Drug Administration: the agency regulating medications and medical devices. No FDA-approved treatments exist specifically for ME/CFS; all pharmacological treatments are off-label.

EMA.label: EMA
EMA.definition: European Medicines Agency: the EU regulatory agency for medicines. Like the FDA, the EMA has no approved ME/CFS-specific treatments.

NO.label: NO
NO.definition: Nitric Oxide: a gaseous signaling molecule involved in vasodilation, neurotransmission, and immune defense. Dysregulated NO metabolism (excess peroxynitrite formation) is central to the NO/ONOO cycle hypothesis of ME/CFS.

Amitriptyline.label: Amitriptyline
Amitriptyline.definition: A tricyclic antidepressant (TCA) used at low doses (5–25 mg) in ME/CFS for sleep initiation, pain, and headache/migraine prophylaxis. One of the most commonly prescribed ME/CFS medications.

Duloxetine.label: Duloxetine
Duloxetine.definition: An SNRI antidepressant also approved for chronic pain conditions (fibromyalgia, neuropathic pain). Used in ME/CFS for pain, mood, and fatigue; dual serotonin/norepinephrine action may address more symptom domains than SSRIs.

Venlafaxine.label: Venlafaxine
Venlafaxine.definition: An SNRI antidepressant with dose-dependent norepinephrine reuptake inhibition (predominantly serotonergic at low doses, dual at higher doses). Used off-label in ME/CFS for pain, fatigue, and mood.

Gabapentin.label: Gabapentin
Gabapentin.definition: A gabapentinoid anticonvulsant acting on voltage-gated calcium channels (α2δ subunit). Used in ME/CFS for neuropathic pain, sleep, and restless legs; may worsen fatigue and brain fog in some patients.

Pregabalin.label: Pregabalin
Pregabalin.definition: A gabapentinoid with higher potency than gabapentin, approved for neuropathic pain, fibromyalgia, and generalized anxiety. Used off-label in ME/CFS for pain; carries risk of dependence.
Pregabalin.rx: Prescription (US, EU); controlled substance in some jurisdictions

Propranolol.label: Propranolol
Propranolol.definition: A non-selective beta-blocker used in ME/CFS/POTS for tachycardia, palpitations, and hyperadrenergic symptoms. Crosses the blood-brain barrier; may reduce sympathetic overactivation and migraine frequency.

Ivabradine.label: Ivabradine
Ivabradine.definition: A heart rate-lowering agent acting on the sinoatrial node If (funny) current. Used in POTS and ME/CFS tachycardia without affecting blood pressure — an advantage over beta-blockers in patients with low or labile BP.

Midodrine.label: Midodrine
Midodrine.definition: An alpha-1 adrenergic agonist vasoconstrictor used in ME/CFS/POTS for orthostatic hypotension and cerebral hypoperfusion. Raises blood pressure in upright posture; should not be taken lying down.

Fludrocortisone.label: Fludrocortisone
Fludrocortisone.definition: A mineralocorticoid steroid that increases sodium retention and plasma volume. Used off-label in ME/CFS/POTS for hypovolemia and orthostatic intolerance; requires monitoring of potassium and blood pressure.

Pyridostigmine.label: Pyridostigmine
Pyridostigmine.definition: An acetylcholinesterase inhibitor that increases synaptic acetylcholine, enhancing parasympathetic (vagal) tone. Used experimentally in ME/CFS/POTS to improve orthostatic tolerance and reduce tachycardia via increased vagal output.

Rituximab.label: Rituximab
Rituximab.definition: A monoclonal anti-CD20 antibody depleting B cells. The phase III RituxME trial (2019) was negative for its primary endpoint, dashing hopes that B-cell depletion treats ME/CFS; however, responders in earlier open-label studies suggest a B-cell-mediated subset may exist.
Rituximab.class: Monoclonal antibody / anti-CD20 / B-cell depleting agent

Methotrexate.label: Methotrexate
Methotrexate.definition: A folate antimetabolite immunosuppressant used in autoimmune diseases. Investigated experimentally in ME/CFS for suspected autoimmune subsets; limited evidence, significant side-effect profile.

Hydroxychloroquine.label: Hydroxychloroquine
Hydroxychloroquine.definition: An antimalarial with immunomodulatory effects (TLR inhibition, reduced antigen presentation). Investigated in ME/CFS for immune dysfunction; known efficacy in SLE/RA suggests utility if the patient has an autoimmune endotype.

Modafinil.label: Modafinil
Modafinil.definition: A wakefulness-promoting agent (eugeroic) acting via dopamine reuptake inhibition. Used off-label in ME/CFS for daytime sleepiness and cognitive dysfunction. Does not treat PEM; may mask exertion limits, increasing crash risk.

Methylphenidate.label: Methylphenidate
Methylphenidate.definition: A CNS stimulant (dopamine/norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor) used for ADHD. Used off-label in ME/CFS for cognitive dysfunction and fatigue; risk of 'borrowing energy' — masking PEM without fixing the underlying energy deficit.

Melatonin.label: Melatonin
Melatonin.definition: The endogenous sleep hormone produced by the pineal gland in response to darkness. Used as a supplement in ME/CFS for sleep onset; may also have antioxidant and mitochondrial-protective effects. Low-dose (0.3–1 mg) may be more physiological than high-dose.
Melatonin.brand: Various supplement brands; Circadin (prescription in EU)
Melatonin.rx: OTC (US); prescription in some EU countries

Acetaminophen.label: Acetaminophen
Acetaminophen.definition: An analgesic and antipyretic with minimal anti-inflammatory activity. Used in ME/CFS for pain and headache; generally well-tolerated but hepatotoxic at supratherapeutic doses.

Ibuprofen.label: Ibuprofen
Ibuprofen.definition: A non-selective NSAID inhibiting COX-1 and COX-2. Used in ME/CFS for pain and inflammation; limited by GI, renal, and cardiovascular side effects with chronic use.

Naproxen.label: Naproxen
Naproxen.definition: A non-selective NSAID with longer half-life than ibuprofen. Used in ME/CFS for pain; carries similar GI/renal risks.

Valacyclovir.label: Valacyclovir
Valacyclovir.definition: A prodrug of acyclovir, an antiviral active against herpesviruses (HSV, VZV, EBV). Used off-label in ME/CFS for suspected chronic herpesvirus reactivation; mixed evidence, with some responders in EBV/HHV-6-driven subsets.
Valacyclovir.class: Antiviral / nucleoside analogue DNA polymerase inhibitor

Acyclovir.label: Acyclovir
Acyclovir.definition: A nucleoside analogue antiviral active against herpesviruses. Lower oral bioavailability than valacyclovir; used in ME/CFS for suspected viral reactivation with limited evidence.
Acyclovir.class: Antiviral / nucleoside analogue DNA polymerase inhibitor

Loratadine.label: Loratadine
Loratadine.definition: A second-generation non-sedating H1 antihistamine. Used in ME/CFS with MCAS or histamine intolerance for allergy-type symptoms (rhinitis, urticaria, pruritus).

Cetirizine.label: Cetirizine
Cetirizine.definition: A second-generation H1 antihistamine with slightly higher sedation potential than loratadine. Used in ME/CFS with MCAS or histamine intolerance symptoms.

Fexofenadine.label: Fexofenadine
Fexofenadine.definition: A second-generation H1 antihistamine with the lowest sedation and anticholinergic profile. Used in ME/CFS for MCAS/histamine intolerance with minimal cognitive side effects.

Famotidine.label: Famotidine
Famotidine.definition: An H2 receptor antagonist reducing stomach acid. Used in ME/CFS with MCAS because gastric H2 receptors are also on mast cells; combined H1+H2 blockade is a standard MCAS treatment approach.

Ketotifen.label: Ketotifen
Ketotifen.definition: A mast cell stabilizer and H1 antihistamine with anti-eosinophil activity. Used in ME/CFS with MCAS for mast cell stabilization; causes significant sedation initially.

Cromolyn.label: Cromolyn
Cromolyn.definition: A mast cell stabilizer preventing degranulation. Oral cromolyn sodium (Gastrocrom) is used in ME/CFS/MCAS for GI mast cell symptoms; poorly absorbed systemically, acting locally in the GI tract.
Cromolyn.rx: OTC (nasal in US); prescription (oral in US)

Ondansetron.label: Ondansetron
Ondansetron.definition: A 5-HT3 receptor antagonist antiemetic. Used in ME/CFS for nausea (common in POTS, MCAS, gastroparesis); favorable side-effect profile with minimal CNS penetration.

Clonidine.label: Clonidine
Clonidine.definition: A central alpha-2 adrenergic agonist reducing sympathetic outflow. Used in hyperadrenergic POTS and ME/CFS for tachycardia, hypertension, and hyperarousal. May worsen fatigue and brain fog.

Guanfacine.label: Guanfacine
Guanfacine.definition: A central alpha-2A adrenergic agonist with preferential prefrontal action. Used off-label in ME/CFS for cognitive dysfunction ('brain fog') and hyperadrenergic symptoms; may improve working memory via prefrontal noradrenergic signaling.

Fluoxetine.label: Fluoxetine
Fluoxetine.definition: A prototypical SSRI with the longest half-life. Used in ME/CFS for comorbid depression, anxiety, and pain; may worsen sleep architecture in some patients via serotonin-mediated sleep disruption.

Bupropion.label: Bupropion
Bupropion.definition: An atypical antidepressant (NDRI) with dopamine and norepinephrine reuptake inhibition. Used off-label in ME/CFS for fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, and ADHD comorbidity; activating profile may help daytime energy but worsen insomnia.

Tramadol.label: Tramadol
Tramadol.definition: A weak opioid analgesic with additional SNRI activity. Used for moderate-to-severe pain in ME/CFS; serotonergic component carries risk of serotonin syndrome when combined with SSRIs/SNRIs.

Prednisone.label: Prednisone
Prednisone.definition: A synthetic glucocorticoid immunosuppressant. Used in ME/CFS anecdotally for inflammatory flares; no RCT evidence. Short-term benefit may occur in immune-mediated subsets, but chronic use is not recommended due to side-effect burden.

Hydrocortisone.label: Hydrocortisone
Hydrocortisone.definition: A physiological glucocorticoid identical to endogenous cortisol. Low-dose hydrocortisone (5–10 mg/day) has been trialed in ME/CFS for HPA axis dysfunction; modest fatigue improvement in some studies but concern about adrenal suppression.

Baclofen.label: Baclofen
Baclofen.definition: A GABA-B receptor agonist muscle relaxant. Used in ME/CFS for muscle spasticity, stiffness, and pain; may improve sleep quality in patients with nocturnal muscle tension.

Cyclobenzaprine.label: Cyclobenzaprine
Cyclobenzaprine.definition: A tricyclic-adjacent muscle relaxant with 5-HT2 receptor antagonism. Used in ME/CFS/fibromyalgia for muscle pain and sleep; sedating effect can be leveraged for sleep initiation.

Celecoxib.label: Celecoxib
Celecoxib.definition: A selective COX-2 inhibitor NSAID with reduced GI toxicity. Used in ME/CFS for inflammation-predominant pain; may carry cardiovascular risk with chronic use.

Losartan.label: Losartan
Losartan.definition: An angiotensin II receptor blocker (ARB) used as an antihypertensive. Investigational in ME/CFS for RAAS modulation and TGF-β reduction (TGF-β overproduction is implicated in ME/CFS).

Suvorexant.label: Suvorexant
Suvorexant.definition: A dual orexin receptor antagonist (DORA) that promotes sleep by blocking wake-promoting orexin signaling. Used in ME/CFS for the 'tired but wired' sleep-onset failure and dysregulated orexin tone.

Mirtazapine.label: Mirtazapine
Mirtazapine.definition: A tetracyclic antidepressant (NaSSA) blocking α2-adrenergic autoreceptors, H1 receptors, and 5-HT2/5-HT3 receptors. Used in ME/CFS for sleep, appetite stimulation, and nausea; strong sedation at lower doses (7.5–15 mg).

Trazodone.label: Trazodone
Trazodone.definition: A serotonin antagonist and reuptake inhibitor (SARI) with strong H1 and α1 blockade at low doses. Used in ME/CFS primarily as a sleep aid (25–100 mg); antidepressant doses (150–400 mg) rarely used due to sedation.

Quetiapine.label: Quetiapine
Quetiapine.definition: An atypical antipsychotic with strong H1 and α1 antagonism at low doses. Used in ME/CFS off-label for severe sleep initiation failure; significant metabolic side-effect profile (weight gain, glucose dysregulation).

Montelukast.label: Montelukast
Montelukast.definition: A leukotriene receptor antagonist (LTRA) blocking cysteinyl leukotrienes (LTC4, LTD4, LTE4). Used in ME/CFS with MCAS for mast-cell-driven respiratory symptoms and systemic inflammation.

Sumatriptan.label: Sumatriptan
Sumatriptan.definition: A 5-HT1B/1D receptor agonist (triptan) for acute migraine. Used in ME/CFS patients with comorbid migraine; cerebral vasoconstriction may address the vascular component of ME/CFS headache.

Omeprazole.label: Omeprazole
Omeprazole.definition: A proton pump inhibitor (PPI) reducing gastric acid secretion. Used in ME/CFS for GERD and GI symptoms; long-term use may reduce nutrient absorption (B12, magnesium).
Omeprazole.rx: OTC (US); prescription or OTC (EU by country)

TRPM3.label: TRPM3
TRPM3.definition: Transient Receptor Potential Melastatin 3: a cation channel activated by heat, pregnenolone sulfate, and osmotic stimuli. Implicated in pain sensation and thermoregulation; TRPM3 dysfunction may contribute to ME/CFS sensory hypersensitivity and temperature dysregulation.

TRPM8.label: TRPM8
TRPM8.definition: Transient Receptor Potential Melastatin 8: the primary cold-sensing ion channel, activated by temperatures below ~25°C and by menthol. Dysregulation may contribute to cold intolerance and temperature perception abnormalities in ME/CFS.

TRPV1.label: TRPV1
TRPV1.definition: Transient Receptor Potential Vanilloid 1: the capsaicin receptor and heat sensor (>43°C). Mediates inflammatory pain and thermal hyperalgesia; TRPV1 sensitization may contribute to widespread pain in ME/CFS/fibromyalgia.

TRPV4.label: TRPV4
TRPV4.definition: Transient Receptor Potential Vanilloid 4: a mechano- and osmo-sensitive cation channel. Involved in vascular regulation, cell volume sensing, and nociception. Implicated in ME/CFS vascular and pain dysfunction.

TRPA1.label: TRPA1
TRPA1.definition: Transient Receptor Potential Ankyrin 1: a chemosensor activated by irritants (mustard oil, acrolein), oxidative stress products (4-HNE, H₂O₂), and cold. TRPA1-mediated detection of endogenous reactive species links oxidative stress to pain in ME/CFS.

PIEZO.label: PIEZO
PIEZO.definition: Mechanosensitive cation channels (PIEZO1, PIEZO2) that convert mechanical force into electrical signals. PIEZO1 mediates vascular shear sensing; PIEZO2 in proprioception and touch. PIEZO dysfunction may contribute to orthostatic intolerance and altered body perception in ME/CFS.

TLR4.label: TLR4
TLR4.definition: Toll-Like Receptor 4: a pattern recognition receptor recognizing bacterial lipopolysaccharide (LPS) and endogenous danger signals (HMGB1, HSPs). TLR4 on microglia drives neuroinflammation; LDN acts in part as a TLR4 antagonist, a key mechanism for its off-label use in ME/CFS.

TLR2.label: TLR2
TLR2.definition: Toll-Like Receptor 2: a pattern recognition receptor recognizing bacterial lipopeptides. TLR2 activation on immune cells contributes to inflammatory cytokine production in ME/CFS.

RAGE.label: RAGE
RAGE.definition: Receptor for Advanced Glycation End-products: a multi-ligand receptor binding AGEs, HMGB1, S100 proteins, and amyloid-β. RAGE activation drives sustained NF-κB signaling and oxidative stress; implicated in ME/CFS neuroinflammation.

HMGB1.label: HMGB1
HMGB1.definition: High Mobility Group Box 1: a nuclear protein that, when released extracellularly, acts as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP), activating TLR4 and RAGE. HMGB1 is elevated in ME/CFS and links cellular stress to neuroinflammation.

ISR.label: ISR
ISR.definition: Integrated Stress Response: a conserved eukaryotic signaling network activated by diverse stresses (ER stress, amino acid deprivation, heme deficiency, viral infection). Converges on eIF2α phosphorylation, attenuating global translation while selectively translating stress-response genes. Chronic ISR activation is hypothesized in ME/CFS and may explain the impaired cellular energy metabolism.

UPR.label: UPR
UPR.definition: Unfolded Protein Response: the ER stress response triggered by accumulation of misfolded proteins. Three branches (PERK, IRE1, ATF6) coordinate translational attenuation, chaperone induction, and ERAD. UPR activation is documented in ME/CFS and may link to metabolic dysfunction.

PERK.label: PERK
PERK.definition: Protein kinase R-like ER Kinase: a UPR sensor that phosphorylates eIF2α to suppress global translation. Prolonged PERK activation drives the ISR; may be chronically activated in ME/CFS, reducing cellular protein synthesis capacity.

IRE1.label: IRE1
IRE1.definition: Inositol-Requiring Enzyme 1: a UPR sensor with endoribonuclease activity that splices XBP1 mRNA. IRE1 activation links ER stress to inflammatory signaling via JNK and NF-κB.

XBP1.label: XBP1
XBP1.definition: X-Box Binding Protein 1: a transcription factor activated by IRE1-mediated splicing that induces ER chaperones and ERAD components. The spliced form (XBP1s) is a master regulator of ER homeostasis.

ATF4.label: ATF4
ATF4.definition: Activating Transcription Factor 4: a stress-responsive transcription factor whose translation is selectively upregulated when eIF2α is phosphorylated. ATF4 controls amino acid metabolism, antioxidant genes, and autophagy; chronic ATF4 activation in ME/CFS may drive metabolic reprogramming.

ATF6.label: ATF6
ATF6.definition: Activating Transcription Factor 6: the third UPR sensor, a transmembrane protein that traffics to the Golgi upon ER stress, where it is cleaved to release an active transcription factor that induces ER chaperones.

CHOP.label: CHOP
CHOP.definition: C/EBP Homologous Protein (also GADD153): a pro-apoptotic transcription factor induced by severe/prolonged ER stress via ATF4. CHOP promotes cell death when UPR adaptation fails; elevated CHOP in ME/CFS may indicate unresolved ER stress.

eIF2.label: eIF2
eIF2.definition: Eukaryotic Initiation Factor 2: a translation initiation factor whose α-subunit phosphorylation by PERK, PKR, GCN2, or HRI halts general protein synthesis. Central to the ISR; eIF2α phosphorylation is a convergence point for multiple stress signals in ME/CFS.

PKR.label: PKR
PKR.definition: Double-stranded RNA-dependent Protein Kinase: an eIF2α kinase activated by viral dsRNA. PKR links antiviral responses to the ISR; chronic low-level PKR activation from persistent viral material could drive ME/CFS metabolic shutdown.

GCN2.label: GCN2
GCN2.definition: General Control Nonderepressible 2: an eIF2α kinase activated by uncharged tRNAs during amino acid deprivation. GCN2 activation may link branched-chain amino acid depletion in ME/CFS to the ISR.

HRI.label: HRI
HRI.definition: Heme-Regulated Inhibitor: an eIF2α kinase activated by heme deficiency, oxidative stress, and mitochondrial dysfunction. HRI may link mitochondrial distress to ISR activation in ME/CFS.

NLRP3.label: NLRP3
NLRP3.definition: NACHT, LRR and PYD domains-containing protein 3: the sensor component of the NLRP3 inflammasome, a multi-protein complex that activates caspase-1 and cleaves pro-IL-1β and pro-IL-18 to their active forms, and cleaves gasdermin D to trigger pyroptosis. NLRP3 inflammasome activation is implicated in ME/CFS neuroinflammation.

STING.label: STING
STING.definition: Stimulator of Interferon Genes: an ER-resident adaptor protein activated by cyclic dinucleotides from cGAS-detected cytosolic DNA. STING drives type I IFN responses; aberrant STING activation from mitochondrial DNA leakage may contribute to ME/CFS interferon signatures.

cGAS.label: cGAS
cGAS.definition: Cyclic GMP-AMP Synthase: a cytosolic DNA sensor producing cGAMP upon detecting double-stranded DNA. cGAS activation by leaked mitochondrial DNA is a candidate mechanism linking mitochondrial damage to innate immune activation in ME/CFS.

RIG-I.label: RIG-I
RIG-I.definition: Retinoic acid-Inducible Gene I: a cytosolic pattern recognition receptor detecting viral RNA. RIG-I/MDA5/MAVS signaling drives type I interferon production; may be chronically activated by persistent viral material in ME/CFS.

MDA5.label: MDA5
MDA5.definition: Melanoma Differentiation-Associated protein 5: a cytosolic RNA sensor recognizing long dsRNA. Together with RIG-I, MDA5 detects viral RNA and signals through MAVS to drive interferon responses.

MAVS.label: MAVS
MAVS.definition: Mitochondrial Antiviral Signaling protein: the mitochondrial adaptor for RIG-I and MDA5. MAVS activation on the mitochondrial outer membrane links antiviral innate immunity to mitochondrial stress signaling.

IRF3.label: IRF3
IRF3.definition: Interferon Regulatory Factor 3: a transcription factor driving type I interferon and chemokine gene expression downstream of cGAS-STING and RIG-I/MAVS pathways. Phosphorylated by TBK1.

IRF7.label: IRF7
IRF7.definition: Interferon Regulatory Factor 7: the master transcription factor for type I interferons. IRF7 amplifies IFN responses in a positive feedback loop; dysregulated IRF7 may sustain the interferon signature in ME/CFS.

TBK1.label: TBK1
TBK1.definition: TANK-Binding Kinase 1: a kinase that phosphorylates IRF3/IRF7 downstream of cGAS-STING and RIG-I/MAVS. TBK1 also regulates autophagy and mitophagy; TBK1 dysfunction may impair mitochondrial quality control in ME/CFS.

BAX.label: BAX
BAX.definition: BCL-2-Associated X protein: a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family protein that forms pores in the mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis, releasing cytochrome c. BAX activation may be increased in ME/CFS metabolic stress.

BCL-2.label: BCL-2
BCL-2.definition: B-Cell Lymphoma 2: the prototypical anti-apoptotic protein that inhibits BAX/BAK pore formation. Reduced BCL-2/BAX ratios indicate a pro-apoptotic shift; may be altered in ME/CFS lymphocytes.

GPCR.label: GPCR
GPCR.definition: G Protein-Coupled Receptor: the largest receptor superfamily, mediating signals from hormones, neurotransmitters, and sensory stimuli through heterotrimeric G proteins. GPCR signaling dysregulation (e.g., adrenergic, histaminergic, serotonergic) is widespread in ME/CFS.

cAMP.label: cAMP
cAMP.definition: Cyclic Adenosine Monophosphate: a ubiquitous second messenger produced by adenylyl cyclase (AC) from ATP, degraded by phosphodiesterases (PDEs). cAMP/PKA signaling regulates energy metabolism, immune function, and synaptic plasticity; cAMP dysregulation is reported in ME/CFS.

PKA.label: PKA
PKA.definition: cAMP-dependent Protein Kinase A: a serine/threonine kinase activated by cAMP. PKA phosphorylates numerous targets including metabolic enzymes, ion channels, and transcription factors (CREB). PKA signaling may be blunted in ME/CFS.

PKC.label: PKC
PKC.definition: Protein Kinase C: a family of serine/threonine kinases activated by diacylglycerol (DAG) and calcium. PKC regulates immune cell activation, vascular tone, and neuronal excitability; PKC dysregulation may contribute to immune dysfunction in ME/CFS.

PI3K.label: PI3K
PI3K.definition: Phosphatidylinositol 3-Kinase: a lipid kinase producing PIP3 from PIP2, activating AKT/mTOR signaling. PI3K/AKT integrates growth factor, insulin, and energy signals; PI3K pathway dysfunction may impair metabolic switching in ME/CFS.

AKT.label: AKT
AKT.definition: AKT (Protein Kinase B): a serine/threonine kinase downstream of PI3K that regulates metabolism, cell survival, and protein synthesis via mTOR and FOXO. Reduced AKT signaling may contribute to ME/CFS metabolic inflexibility and immune dysfunction.

PLC.label: PLC
PLC.definition: Phospholipase C: an enzyme cleaving PIP2 into DAG and IP3 upon GPCR or RTK activation. IP3 triggers calcium release from ER stores; DAG activates PKC. PLC-γ is critical for T cell and mast cell activation in ME/CFS.

DAG.label: DAG
DAG.definition: Diacylglycerol: a lipid second messenger produced by PLC, activating PKC. DAG is also a metabolic intermediate linking lipid metabolism to signaling.

IP3.label: IP3
IP3.definition: Inositol 1,4,5-Trisphosphate: a second messenger produced by PLC that opens IP3 receptors on the ER, releasing calcium into the cytoplasm. IP3-mediated calcium signaling is essential for immune cell activation and neurotransmitter release.

STAT1.label: STAT1
STAT1.definition: Signal Transducer and Activator of Transcription 1: a transcription factor activated by IFN-γ and type I interferons via JAK phosphorylation. STAT1 drives antiviral and pro-inflammatory gene programs; STAT1 activation may be elevated in ME/CFS.

STAT3.label: STAT3
STAT3.definition: Signal Transducer and Activator of Transcription 3: a transcription factor activated by IL-6 family cytokines and growth factors. STAT3 regulates inflammation, metabolism, and Th17 differentiation; STAT3/Th17 skewing is reported in ME/CFS.

JAK.label: JAK
JAK.definition: Janus Kinase: a family of tyrosine kinases (JAK1, JAK2, JAK3, TYK2) that phosphorylate STAT proteins downstream of cytokine receptors. JAK-STAT signaling is central to immune activation in ME/CFS.

AP-1.label: AP-1
AP-1.definition: Activator Protein 1: a dimeric transcription factor (c-Fos/c-Jun) activated by MAPK signaling (JNK, ERK). AP-1 drives pro-inflammatory and stress-response genes; AP-1 activation links MAPK dysregulation to inflammation in ME/CFS.

CREB.label: CREB
CREB.definition: cAMP Response Element-Binding protein: a transcription factor phosphorylated by PKA, CaMK, and MAPK pathways. CREB regulates synaptic plasticity, metabolism, and cell survival; altered CREB signaling may contribute to ME/CFS cognitive dysfunction.

NFAT.label: NFAT
NFAT.definition: Nuclear Factor of Activated T-cells: a calcium/calcineurin-activated transcription factor driving T-cell activation genes (IL-2, IFN-γ, TNF-α). NFAT also regulates cardiac hypertrophy and neuronal function; NFAT dysregulation is reported in ME/CFS immune studies.

CaMK.label: CaMK
CaMK.definition: Calcium/Calmodulin-dependent Kinase: a family of serine/threonine kinases activated by calcium-bound calmodulin. CaMKII is critical for learning, memory, and cardiac function; calcium signaling dysfunction in ME/CFS may impair CaMK-dependent processes.

SERCA.label: SERCA
SERCA.definition: Sarco/Endoplasmic Reticulum Calcium ATPase: the pump that transports calcium from the cytoplasm back into the ER/SR. SERCA dysfunction leads to ER calcium depletion, ER stress, and impaired calcium signaling — all documented in ME/CFS.

IP3R.label: IP3R
IP3R.definition: IP3 Receptor: an ER calcium release channel gated by IP3. IP3R-mediated calcium release couples surface receptor activation to intracellular calcium signals driving immune function and metabolism.

VDAC.label: VDAC
VDAC.definition: Voltage-Dependent Anion Channel: the primary channel in the mitochondrial outer membrane, controlling metabolite flux (ATP, ADP, NADH) between mitochondria and cytoplasm. VDAC dysfunction impairs mitochondrial-cytosolic energy exchange in ME/CFS.

MCU.label: MCU
MCU.definition: Mitochondrial Calcium Uniporter: the primary channel for calcium uptake into the mitochondrial matrix. MCU-mediated mitochondrial calcium regulates ATP production and apoptosis; MCU dysfunction may impair mitochondrial energetics in ME/CFS.

OXPHOS.label: OXPHOS
OXPHOS.definition: Oxidative Phosphorylation: the mitochondrial process coupling electron transport chain activity to ATP synthesis via Complex V (ATP synthase). Impaired OXPHOS is one of the most consistently replicated metabolic abnormalities in ME/CFS.

ETC.label: ETC
ETC.definition: Electron Transport Chain: the series of mitochondrial complexes (I–IV) that transfer electrons from NADH/FADH₂ to oxygen, pumping protons to drive ATP synthesis. Complex I and IV dysfunction are reported in ME/CFS.

PDH.label: PDH
PDH.definition: Pyruvate Dehydrogenase: the enzyme complex converting pyruvate to acetyl-CoA, linking glycolysis to the TCA cycle. PDH is inhibited by PDK phosphorylation; PDK/PDH ratio dysregulation is a candidate mechanism for metabolic inflexibility in ME/CFS.
PDH.also: PDC (Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Complex); regulated by PDK1–4 and PDP1–2

PDK.label: PDK
PDK.definition: Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Kinase: kinases (PDK1–4) that phosphorylate and inactivate PDH, reducing glucose oxidation. PDK upregulation may lock ME/CFS cells into a glycolytic metabolic state even when oxygen is available.

GLUT1.label: GLUT1
GLUT1.definition: Glucose Transporter 1: the primary glucose transporter at the blood-brain barrier and in erythrocytes. Reduced GLUT1 expression or function could limit cerebral glucose availability in ME/CFS, contributing to brain fog.

GLUT3.label: GLUT3
GLUT3.definition: Glucose Transporter 3: the primary neuronal glucose transporter with high affinity (low Km). Essential for maintaining neuronal energy; GLUT3 dysfunction could contribute to ME/CFS neurocognitive symptoms.

GLUT4.label: GLUT4
GLUT4.definition: Glucose Transporter 4: the insulin-responsive glucose transporter in muscle and adipose tissue. Insulin resistance or impaired GLUT4 translocation may reduce muscle glucose uptake in ME/CFS.

CPT1.label: CPT1
CPT1.definition: Carnitine Palmitoyltransferase 1: the rate-limiting enzyme for mitochondrial fatty acid oxidation, transporting long-chain fatty acyl-CoA into mitochondria. CPT1 dysfunction may impair fatty acid utilization in ME/CFS, forcing reliance on glycolysis.

CPT2.label: CPT2
CPT2.definition: Carnitine Palmitoyltransferase 2: the inner mitochondrial membrane enzyme reconstituting fatty acyl-CoA from acylcarnitine. CPT2 deficiency/impaired activity may contribute to lipid metabolism abnormalities in ME/CFS.

GAPDH.label: GAPDH
GAPDH.definition: Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate Dehydrogenase: a glycolytic enzyme also functioning as an RNA-binding protein and redox sensor. GAPDH is oxidatively modified in ME/CFS, potentially impairing both glycolysis and its signaling functions.

MCT1.label: MCT1
MCT1.definition: Monocarboxylate Transporter 1: transports lactate, pyruvate, and ketone bodies across cell membranes. MCT1 at the blood-brain barrier imports ketones and lactate as alternative brain fuels; MCT1 dysfunction could worsen cerebral energy deficits in ME/CFS.

UCP.label: UCP
UCP.definition: Uncoupling Protein: mitochondrial inner membrane proteins (UCP1–5) that dissipate the proton gradient as heat, reducing ATP synthesis. UCP2/3 upregulation in ME/CFS may reduce mitochondrial efficiency as an adaptive antioxidant response.

HO-1.label: HO-1
HO-1.definition: Heme Oxygenase-1: the inducible enzyme degrading heme to biliverdin, carbon monoxide, and free iron. HO-1 is a key NRF2-target antioxidant and anti-inflammatory gene; HO-1 induction may be impaired in ME/CFS despite oxidative stress.

NQO1.label: NQO1
NQO1.definition: NAD(P)H Quinone Dehydrogenase 1: an NRF2-target enzyme reducing quinones to hydroquinones, preventing redox cycling and oxidative damage. Reduced NQO1 activity is reported in ME/CFS.

TXNRD.label: TXNRD
TXNRD.definition: Thioredoxin Reductase: a selenoprotein enzyme reducing oxidized thioredoxin using NADPH. Essential for ribonucleotide reductase activity (DNA synthesis) and antioxidant defense; TXNRD dysfunction may impair redox balance in ME/CFS.

PPAR.label: PPAR
PPAR.definition: Peroxisome Proliferator-Activated Receptor: a nuclear receptor family (PPARα, β/δ, γ) regulating lipid metabolism, inflammation, and mitochondrial biogenesis. PPAR agonists have been proposed as experimental ME/CFS therapies targeting metabolic dysfunction.

FOXO1.label: FOXO1
FOXO1.definition: Forkhead Box O1: a transcription factor regulating metabolism, antioxidant defense, and autophagy. AKT phosphorylation excludes FOXO from the nucleus; impaired FOXO signaling may reduce stress-resistance programs in ME/CFS.

FOXO3.label: FOXO3
FOXO3.definition: Forkhead Box O3: a longevity-associated transcription factor activating antioxidant, DNA repair, and autophagy genes. FOXO3 activity is regulated by AMPK and SIRT1; FOXO3 dysfunction may impair cellular resilience in ME/CFS.

NRF1.label: NRF1
NRF1.definition: Nuclear Respiratory Factor 1: a transcription factor coordinating nuclear-encoded mitochondrial gene expression with mitochondrial biogenesis. NRF1 works with PGC-1α and TFAM; NRF1 impairment may contribute to the mitochondrial phenotype in ME/CFS.

TFAM.label: TFAM
TFAM.definition: Mitochondrial Transcription Factor A: the key regulator of mitochondrial DNA transcription, replication, and packaging. TFAM is downstream of PGC-1α/NRF1; reduced TFAM may impair mitochondrial gene expression and biogenesis in ME/CFS.

PARP.label: PARP
PARP.definition: Poly(ADP-Ribose) Polymerase: a DNA damage-sensing enzyme that consumes NAD+. Excessive PARP activation depletes cellular NAD+, impairing glycolysis and mitochondrial function — a candidate mechanism for ME/CFS energy failure.

D1R.label: D1R
D1R.definition: Dopamine D1 Receptor: a Gs-coupled receptor activating cAMP/PKA signaling. D1R in the prefrontal cortex supports working memory; D1R dysfunction may contribute to ME/CFS cognitive symptoms.

5-HT3.label: 5-HT3
5-HT3.definition: 5-HT3 receptor: the only ligand-gated ion channel among serotonin receptors. Mediates nausea and vomiting; 5-HT3 antagonists (ondansetron) are used for ME/CFS-related nausea.

AT1R.label: AT1R
AT1R.definition: Angiotensin II Type 1 Receptor: the primary receptor mediating angiotensin II effects (vasoconstriction, aldosterone release, inflammation). AT1R overactivation contributes to oxidative stress and vascular dysfunction in ME/CFS.

nAChR.label: nAChR
nAChR.definition: Nicotinic Acetylcholine Receptor: a ligand-gated ion channel activated by acetylcholine and nicotine. Neuronal nAChRs (α7, α4β2) regulate cognition, attention, and autonomic tone; nAChR dysfunction may contribute to cognitive and autonomic symptoms in ME/CFS.

OSA.label: OSA
OSA.definition: Obstructive Sleep Apnea: recurrent upper airway collapse during sleep causing intermittent hypoxia and sleep fragmentation. OSA is a common comorbidity and must be excluded in ME/CFS patients with unrefreshing sleep.

RLS.label: RLS
RLS.definition: Restless Legs Syndrome (Willis-Ekbom disease): an irresistible urge to move the legs, often with uncomfortable sensations, worsening at rest and at night. Highly prevalent in ME/CFS and contributes to sleep-onset difficulties.

NREM.label: NREM
NREM.definition: Non-Rapid Eye Movement sleep: sleep stages N1–N3 (light to deep/slow-wave sleep). Reduced N3 (slow-wave sleep) and alpha intrusion into NREM are characteristic EEG findings in ME/CFS.

REM.label: REM
REM.definition: Rapid Eye Movement sleep: the sleep stage associated with vivid dreaming, muscle atonia, and memory consolidation. REM sleep abnormalities (timing, density, fragmentation) are reported in ME/CFS.

SWS.label: SWS
SWS.definition: Slow-Wave Sleep (N3): the deepest non-REM sleep stage, characterized by high-amplitude delta waves. SWS is critical for glymphatic clearance, growth hormone secretion, and tissue repair. Reduced SWS and alpha-delta intrusion are hallmarks of ME/CFS sleep pathology.

PSG.label: PSG
PSG.definition: Polysomnography: comprehensive overnight sleep study recording EEG, EOG, EMG, ECG, respiratory effort, airflow, and oxygen saturation. PSG in ME/CFS reveals reduced sleep efficiency, alpha intrusion, frequent arousals, and reduced slow-wave sleep.

WASO.label: WASO
WASO.definition: Wake After Sleep Onset: total time spent awake after initially falling asleep. Elevated WASO is typical in ME/CFS, reflecting sleep maintenance insomnia and frequent nocturnal arousals.

TST.label: TST
TST.definition: Total Sleep Time: the actual time spent asleep during a sleep period. TST may be normal in quantity but severely deficient in quality in ME/CFS.

PCA.label: PCA
PCA.definition: Principal Component Analysis: a dimensionality reduction technique identifying orthogonal components explaining maximal variance in multivariate data. Used in ME/CFS metabolomics and cytokine studies to identify latent biological dimensions.

t-SNE.label: t-SNE
t-SNE.definition: t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding: a non-linear dimensionality reduction method preserving local structure in high-dimensional data. Used in ME/CFS for visualizing immune cell populations and metabolic profiles.

UMAP.label: UMAP
UMAP.definition: Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection: a dimensionality reduction technique preserving both local and global data structure, often preferred over t-SNE. Used in ME/CFS single-cell and metabolomics analyses.

LASSO.label: LASSO
LASSO.definition: Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator: a regression method performing variable selection and regularization. Used in ME/CFS biomarker discovery to identify minimal sets of predictive analytes.

AUC.label: AUC
AUC.definition: Area Under the receiver operating characteristic Curve: a measure of diagnostic test performance (0.5 = random; 1.0 = perfect). ME/CFS biomarker panels aim for AUC > 0.80 for clinical utility.

ROC.label: ROC
ROC.definition: Receiver Operating Characteristic curve: plots sensitivity vs 1-specificity across test thresholds. Used to evaluate ME/CFS diagnostic biomarkers and classifiers.

OR.label: OR
OR.definition: Odds Ratio: a measure of association between exposure and outcome in case-control studies. Used in ME/CFS epidemiology to quantify risk factors.

RR.label: RR
RR.definition: Relative Risk (Risk Ratio): the ratio of outcome probability in exposed vs unexposed groups. Used in ME/CFS cohort studies.

HR.label: HR
HR.definition: Hazard Ratio: the ratio of instantaneous event rates between groups in survival analysis. In ME/CFS, HR can describe recovery or deterioration rates over time.

CI.label: CI
CI.definition: Confidence Interval: a range of values likely to contain the true population parameter. 95% CI is the standard; wide CIs in ME/CFS studies often reflect small sample sizes.

ANOVA.label: ANOVA
ANOVA.definition: Analysis of Variance: a statistical test comparing means across multiple groups. Widely used in ME/CFS biomarker studies to compare patient subgroups vs controls.

ANCOVA.label: ANCOVA
ANCOVA.definition: Analysis of Covariance: ANOVA adjusted for covariates (e.g., age, sex, BMI). Important in ME/CFS studies to control for confounding variables.

CONSORT.label: CONSORT
CONSORT.definition: Consolidated Standards of Reporting Trials: a guideline for reporting RCTs. ME/CFS trials are assessed against CONSORT criteria for methodological quality.

PRISMA.label: PRISMA
PRISMA.definition: Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses: a guideline and checklist for systematic reviews. Used extensively in ME/CFS evidence synthesis.

GRADE.label: GRADE
GRADE.definition: Grading of Recommendations Assessment, Development and Evaluation: a framework for rating certainty of evidence and strength of recommendations. NICE ME/CFS guidelines used GRADE methodology.

CRISPR.label: CRISPR
CRISPR.definition: Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats: a gene-editing technology using Cas9 nuclease guided by a single guide RNA. Used experimentally to study ME/CFS-associated gene variants in cell models.

WT.label: WT
WT.definition: Wild-Type: the normal, unmodified genetic background in experimental models. Used as the control in ME/CFS-related molecular studies.

siRNA.label: siRNA
siRNA.definition: Small Interfering RNA: double-stranded RNA molecules that silence gene expression via RNA interference. Used to study gene function in ME/CFS cellular models.

NAFLD.label: NAFLD
NAFLD.definition: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease: hepatic fat accumulation without significant alcohol consumption. Associated with metabolic syndrome and may co-occur in ME/CFS patients with metabolic comorbidities.
NAFLD.also: Now also referred to as MASLD (Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease)

CAD.label: CAD
CAD.definition: Coronary Artery Disease: atherosclerotic narrowing of coronary arteries. ME/CFS is not a risk factor, but overlapping chest pain/fatigue symptoms require differential diagnosis.

CVD.label: CVD
CVD.definition: Cardiovascular Disease: the umbrella term for diseases of the heart and blood vessels. ME/CFS autonomic dysfunction may mimic or exacerbate CVD symptoms.

DVT.label: DVT
DVT.definition: Deep Vein Thrombosis: a blood clot in a deep vein, typically in the leg. Immobility in severe ME/CFS increases DVT risk.

PE.label: PE
